description: A vertical, portrait-orientation 'Labor News' headline card from the U.S. Department of Labor. The upper two-thirds is a color photograph of a large American flag flying from a tall flagpole over the terraced, multicolored rock walls of an open-pit mine, with mine buildings visible on the rim and a blue sky background. The lower third transitions into a dark navy panel carrying the text. A blue rectangular banner with white italic serif text reads 'LABOR NEWS' (accompanied by a few vertical accent bars), and below it in white bold serif type: 'US Department of Labor announces injury rates in the mining industry fell to an all-time low in 2025.' A faint embossed DOL agency seal watermark is visible behind the text, and 'DOL.GOV' appears in the lower-right corner. The design mimics a press-release/news graphic, pairing patriotic mining imagery (flag over an American mine) with a positive safety statistic. The messaging technique is achievement framing crediting the Trump administration with both revitalizing mining and improving worker safety. Target audience is workers, the mining industry, and the public, signaling pro-industry and pro-worker results.
notable_text: LABOR NEWS; US Department of Labor announces injury rates in the mining industry fell to an all-time low in 2025; DOL.GOV
